_comment: Frame-level segmentation of how_to_train_your_nailong.mp4. Times are milliseconds from t=0 of the original mp4. Filled by tools/probe_segments.py and consumed by Media/VideoController.

_field_doc.stare_start_ms / stare_end_ms: The 'looking-at-viewer' segment that loops while we wait for the laugh trigger.
_field_doc.laugh_trigger_frame_ms: Frame at which we cut over to the laugh segment. Should equal stare_end_ms so the cut is invisible.
_field_doc.laugh_segment_end_ms: End of the laugh playback. null = play until natural end of file.
_field_doc.pause_after_stare_ms_range: Hold-on-last-frame duration (uniform random) at the end of forward stare, before reverse begins.
_field_doc.pause_before_stare_ms_range: Hold-on-first-frame duration (uniform random) at the end of reverse stare, before next forward begins.
_field_doc.reverse_source: Pre-generated reverse video. Now contains ONLY the stare segment reversed (~1s, no audio) instead of the whole 15.4s clip — see tools/make_reverse_video.sh for why this matters at first source-swap.
_field_doc.source_hevc_original: Untouched HEVC original from upstream. Currently unused at runtime; kept on disk for reference / fallback. Switch source to this URI if you re-encode and want HEVC.
_field_doc.reverse_kind: Documents what reverse_source contains: 'stare_segment_only' (current) plays 0 → stare_duration with no seek; 'full_clip' (legacy) requires VideoController to seek to (duration - stare_end_ms).
